title: The Essays of Warren Buffett: Timeless lessons for US ...
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > **4. Capital allocation as a defining skill**
Content:
In The Essays of Warren Buffett, capital allocation emerges as the most important responsibility of management. Operational excellence matters, but Buffett consistently argues that how capital is deployed ultimately determines long-term results. A well-run business can still destroy value if it reinvests earnings at ordinary returns, overpays for acquisitions, or pursues growth for its own sake.
Buffett's record at Berkshire Hathaway shows how this discipline plays out in practice. He evaluates every use of capital against clear hurdle rates and realistic alternatives. Arbitrage opportunities, for example, played a [meaningful role in Berkshire Hathaway's](https://www.investmentnews.com/equities/have-we-reached-peak-berkshire-hathaway/262755) results for long periods. These opportunities were judged not on activity but on whether they offered returns superior to cash or short-term Treasuries after accounting fo

title: Warren Buffett - Wikipedia
excerpts:
Section Title: Early life and education > Bibliography > Books about Buffett
Content:
In October 2008, *USA Today* reported at least 47 books were in print with Buffett's name in the title. The article quoted the CEO of Borders Books , George Jones, as saying that the only other living persons named in as many book titles were U.S. presidents, world political figures and the Dalai Lama . <sup>[[ 249 ]]()</sup> Buffett said that his own personal favorite is a collection of his essays called *The Essays of Warren Buffett* , <sup>[[ 250 ]]()</sup> which he described as "a coherent rearrangement of ideas from my annual report letters". <sup>[[ 249 ]]()</sup>
Books or publications by Buffett:

title: A Message From Warren E. Buffett
excerpts:
**A Message From Warren E. Buffett, CEO of Berkshire Hathaway Inc.**
Dear Reader,
You probably know that I don't make stock recommendations. However, I
have two thoughts regarding your personal expenditures that can save you
real money. I'm suggesting that you call on the services of two subsidiaries
of Berkshire: GEICO and Borsheim's.
I estimate that about 40% of all auto drivers in the country can save
money by insuring with GEICO. The figure is not 100% because insurers differ
in their underwriting judgments, with some favoring drivers who live in
certain geographical areas and work in certain occupations more than GEICO
does. I believe, however, that GEICO more frequently offers the low price
than does any other national carrier selling insurance to all comers. You
can quickly find out whether you can save money by going to [www.geico.com](http://www.geico.com/) or by calling 800-555-2756.
 ... 
|  | Sincerely, |
|  |  |
|  | Warren E. Buffett |

title: Qui Est Warren Buffett ? L'homme Et Ses Investissements - The Poor Swiss
excerpts:
Section Title: Qui est Warren Buffett ? L’homme et ses investissements
Content:
[Baptiste Wicht](https://thepoorswiss.com/fr/author/baptiste/) | **Mis à jour:** 02 avril 2026 |
[Investir](https://thepoorswiss.com/fr/investir/) » [Fondamentaux de l'investissement](https://thepoorswiss.com/fr/fondamentaux-investissement/)
( [Information](https://thepoorswiss.com/fr/privacy-policy/) : certains des liens ci-dessous peuvent être des liens d'affiliation)
Si vous suivez des blogs ou des podcasts sur les finances personnelles, vous avez probablement entendu le nom de Warren Buffett. Il est très connu dans la communauté. Warren Buffett est un investisseur très prospère.
Il a fait fortune à partir de rien. Le fait qu’il soit l’une des personnes les plus riches du monde contribue également à sa notoriété. C’est la première raison pour laquelle il est célèbre dans la communauté.
